sources: Prefecture for Economic Affairs 2026 Fiscal Strategy

real_estate_investments.name: Global Real Estate and Asset Management

apostolic_mission_curia.name: Curial Operations and Global Apostolic Mission

personnel_and_social_security.name: Salaries, Benefits, and Pension Fund Stabilization

charity_and_humanitarian_aid.name: Global Charitable Works and Emergency Aid

2026.methodology: The 2026 forecast assumes a return to baseline activity levels following the 2025 Jubilee. Total income is projected based on a 35% decrease in museum traffic compared to the peak year. Spending reflects the new administration's focus on long-term pension fund solvency and a reduction in extraordinary security costs. The projected deficit of 18,700,000 USD is expected to be covered by asset liquidations as part of the ongoing transparency and sustainability reform initiated by the Secretariat for the Economy. Figures converted from EUR to USD at a projected rate of 1.10.

sources: Prefecture for the Economic Affairs Forecast

personnel_and_pensions.name: Vatican Staff Salaries and Pension Fund

charity_and_aid.name: Dicastery for the Service of Charity

2025.methodology: Figures for 2025 reflect the significant impact of the Jubilee year. Income is projected with a 13% increase compared to 2024 due to record Vatican Museum attendance and commemorative sales. Spending shows a planned deficit of 22,000,000 USD to cover extraordinary maintenance and security costs associated with the Holy Year events. Methodology utilizes the official 2025 budget framework approved by the Pope. Converted to USD at a 1.10 rate.

sources: Administration of the Patrimony of the Apostolic See (APSA) Annual Report
Institute for the Works of Religion (IOR) Annual Report

vatican_city_state_contribution.name: Contributions from Vatican City State Governor's Office

2024.methodology: Data compiled from the 2024 Consolidated Financial Statement of the Holy See. Total operating income reported at 1.353 billion USD. Spending calculated based on total operating expenses adjusted for the reported 1.76 million USD surplus. Percentages for income sources and spending categories are derived from the Secretariat for the Economy's breakdown excluding hospital entities to reflect the core mission budget. Converted from EUR to USD.
